title: Ocimum vanderystii
taxonomy: Ocimum vanderystii is classified within the plant kingdom as follows: it is a species of flowering plant in the mint family (Lamiaceae), and its genus Ocimum is part of the order Lamiales. More specifically, Ocimum vanderystii belongs to the family Lamiaceae, which includes many other herbs and flowering plants. Within this family, Ocimum vanderystii is part of the subfamily Nepetoideae. This species is native to tropical Africa, where it grows in a variety of habitats. Its classification also reflects its evolutionary relationships with other plants in the same genus and family.

intro: Ocimum vanderystii, also known as African basil, is a perennial plant species in the family Lamiaceae, native to tropical Africa. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its various health benefits, including antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties, which have been attributed to its ability to aid in wound healing, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Ocimum vanderystii include essential oils, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Ocimum vanderystii is commonly prepared as a tea or infusion, as well as being used in traditional remedies such as a topical paste for skin conditions.
